Article Title: How to get your CIBIL credit score without a PAN card
Article Text:
Your credit score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity.

What is a CIBIL score?

How to get your CIBIL Credit score without a PAN Card

Factors affecting your CIBIL score

CIBIL credit score : Understanding your creditworthiness is pivotal in today's financial landscape, especially when seeking loans or credit cards. The Credit Information Bureau (India) Limited, commonly known as CIBIL, plays an important role in this regard by providing credit scores to borrowers.According to ET, credit score helps banks make informed decisions about extending credit cards and loans. Your credit history influences your CIBIL score , which is calculated using a special method.Your CIBIL score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity. Ranging from 300 to 900, a higher score indicates a stronger likelihood of loan approval. Banks and financial institutions use this score to assess the risk associated with lending money.1. Go to the official CIBIL website and click on 'Personal CIBIL score,' then 'Get Your Free CIBIL score.' Follow the link provided and fill in all the required details.2. If you don’t have a PAN Card, you can use an alternative identification such as Passport, Voter ID, Driver’s License, or Ration Card. Enter the number of the chosen ID.3. Provide your date of birth, PIN code, and select your state. Finally, enter your mobile number and click on ‘Accept and Continue’.4. Authenticate your identity by entering the OTP (One-Time Password) delivered to your mobile device on the next screen. Click 'Continue' to proceed.5. Choose whether you want to link the device with your account. Select 'Yes' or 'No' based on your preference.6. Upon successful registration, you will see a page confirming that 'You have successfully registered!'7. Click on ‘Go to Dashboard’ to access and view your CIBIL score.The factors influencing the new CIBIL score, as stated on the CIBIL website, include:1. Depth of credit: This refers to the duration of your existing credit history, calculated from the opening date of your oldest credit account.2. Long-term trend of outstanding balances: The overall trend of outstanding balances over time affects your score.3. Transaction history on credit cards: How you manage transactions on your credit cards impacts your score.4. Ratio of actual repayment amount to total amount due: This ratio indicates your repayment behavior and influences your score accordingly.5. Number of new accounts opened and number of accounts closed: Opening or closing accounts can affect your score, so managing these actions wisely is important.
Article Summary: Your CIBIL score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity. Ranging from 300 to 900, a higher score indicates a stronger likelihood of loan approval. Banks and financial institutions use this score to assess the risk associated with lending money.

Article Title: Making waves underground: India's first undersea rail tunnel takes shape
Article Text:
One of India's biggest tunnel boring machines



A 39-metre-deep launch shaft



Safety systems and waterproof design



India's first undersea rail tunnel has moved a step closer to reality, with a giant Tunnel Boring Machine (TBM) beginning its journey beneath Thane Creek on Saturday for the Mumbai–Ahmedabad Bullet Train Project.The launch of the second machine marks the start of excavation on the 7-km undersea stretch, the first of its kind for any rail corridor in the country. The second TBM has started tunnelling from Sawli (Ghansoli) towards Vikhroli in Maharashtra. The undersea section forms part of a 10-km tunnel drive, with 7 km running below Thane Creek.The Mumbai–Ahmedabad Bullet Train Project includes a 21-km underground tunnel, of which 16 km between Sawli (Ghansoli) and Bandra Kurla Complex (BKC) will be excavated using Tunnel Boring Machines. Work is already underway on another stretch after the first TBM began its 6-km drive from Vikhroli towards BKC on July 5, 2026. The remaining 5 km of the underground section has already been completed using the New Austrian Tunnelling Method (NATM).Designed for challenging ground conditions, the newly deployed machine is among the largest Tunnel Boring Machines ever used for rail tunnel construction in India. Its cutterhead measures 13.6 metres across—roughly the height of a four-storey building, while the machine weighs 3,200 tonnes, equivalent to around 500 Asian elephants.Stretching 96 metres in length, it is almost as long as a football pitch.The TBM is equipped with a cutterhead, main bearing, jaw crusher, erector, main shield, tail shield and four specialised gantries that support tunnelling operations. Configured as a Mixshield-type, semi-automatic, slurry-based machine, it uses a pressurised bentonite slurry circuit to stabilise the tunnel face during excavation. The technology has been selected for the Mumbai suburban section because it offers better control over ground settlement while minimising disruption on the surface.It also features a Semi-Continuous Advance (SCA) system, allowing tunnel excavation and segment ring installation to take place simultaneously. This enables faster progress while maintaining safety during construction.Launching the machine required the construction of a 39-metre-deep shaft at Sawli, nearly the depth of a 12-storey building below ground level. Since there was limited space inside the shaft, the TBM was lowered in separate sections. The gantries were placed first and pushed into the already completed NATM tunnel, followed by the main shield and cutterhead.Behind the machine, four double-storey backup gantries, each measuring about 18 to 20 metres, carry systems such as the roller crusher, slurry pumps, hydraulic equipment, operator cabin, grouting systems, power units, emergency refuge chambers, workshop area, cable trays and hose reels.The TBM is fitted with a real-time multi-gas monitoring system to detect methane, oxygen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide. Fire safety measures include automatic fire detection and extinguishing systems, a safety water curtain and an active sprinkler network along the designated escape route.The launch shaft is supported by infrastructure including a water treatment plant, slurry treatment plant, bentonite storage tanks, a dedicated power substation, backup generators, a ready-mix concrete plant for grouting, a slurry transport system, a sewage treatment plant and other logistics facilities.The project also incorporates a real-time monitoring system to track ground movement and protect nearby structures. Instruments such as Surface Settlement Points, Optical Displacement Sensors, Tilt Meters, Bi-Reflective Targets, strain gauges and seismographs are being used throughout the excavation.The tunnel being excavated using TBMs is designed as a fully waterproof structure. Continuous monitoring systems will track structural performance, groundwater behaviour and overall construction safety. To prevent water ingress, the tunnel lining will use double-layer Ethylene Propylene Diene Monomer (EPDM) gaskets along with hydrophilic seals to ensure long-term durability and safety.
Article Summary: The Mumbai–Ahmedabad Bullet Train Project includes a 21-km underground tunnel, of which 16 km will be excavated using Tunnel Boring Machines. The second TBM has started tunnelling from Sawli (Ghansoli) towards Vikhroli in Maharashtra. The undersea section forms part of a 10-km tunnel drive, with 7 km running below Thane Creek.

Over the past decade, Turkey has quietly become the world's busiest destination for hair restoration. The reasons are not mysterious: a dense concentration of specialised surgeons, a healthcare infrastructure built around international patients, and treatment packages that often cost a fraction of what the same procedure runs in Western Europe or North America. Add a city like Istanbul, where you can recover between two continents, with the Bosphorus on one side and centuries of history on the other, and it becomes clear why so many people turn a medical appointment into a genuine trip.But choosing a clinic from abroad is harder than choosing a hotel. You are trusting a team you have never met with a result you will carry for the rest of your life. This guide ranks ten of the most established hair transplant clinics in Istanbul and pairs the list with practical travel advice, so you can plan both the procedure and the days around it with confidence.The industry's premier medical R&D hub and pioneer of ultra-dense hair restoration.A multidisciplinary board of surgeons and biomedical researchers with over 15 years of combined clinical experience. Vera Clinic is not just a hair transplant centre; it is a recognised research and development institution that actively develops the industry's next-generation technologies. The team is behind innovations, including theCVD lab-grown diamond-tipped instrument for precision micro-incisions andfor maximum density. Named Best Hair Transplant Clinic in Turkey by the European Award in Medicine (2021) , and home to, which trains physicians and runs ongoing clinical research, the clinic gives patients access to patented, next-generation methods inside a five-star facility.Expert-led aesthetic restoration overseen by a Cleveland Clinic-trained plastic surgeon.Directed by Dr Ekrem Keskin, with extensive verified acclaim across major international medical review platforms.Capital Hair Center places a board-certified plastic surgeon at the helm of every procedure, bringing genuine precision to hairline design and facial harmony. Sapphire FUE and DHI are complemented by proprietary treatments like OzonFect and needle-free anaesthesia. A strong option for patients who want their transplant handled with the rigour of reconstructive surgery.A boutique clinic focused on personalised treatment and individual hairline design.Founded in 2016 by Şule Ölmez and supported by an experienced medical team.Unlike larger clinics that perform a high volume of procedures, Sule Hair Transplant emphasises a more personalised patient experience. The clinic offers Sapphire FUE and DHI techniques, with treatment plans tailored to each patient's goals and hair characteristics. Patients who value direct communication, detailed consultation, and a boutique environment often find the clinic's approach appealing. Its all-inclusive packages and structured aftercare programme also make the process straightforward for international visitors.A boutique approach pairing AI mapping technology with low daily patient volumes.A boutique team headed by Dr Merve Kaya.Este Favor focuses on lower daily intakes, so each case receives dedicated attention, pairing AI-assisted planning with the now-familiar combination of treatment plus travel coordination. It is a careful, detail-oriented option for medical tourists who want a smaller-scale experience.A long-standing, multidisciplinary institution with two decades of experience.Boasting a 4.8-star rating from thousands of verified reviews and global recognition in outlets.Aslı Tarcan Clinic draws on two decades of experience, covering Sapphire FUE, DHI, and Robotic DHI, with optional PRP and stem cell therapies. Multilingual staff, VIP transfers, and structured aftercare make it a solid choice for patients seeking a review-backed clinic with a long track record and all-inclusive setup.A straightforward, package-based experience for international medical tourists.An established Istanbul provider working in the international medical tourism space.Medart bundles the procedure with all the practical essentials, consultation, transfers, accommodation guidance, and aftercare that overseas patients look for. It's a strong pick for those comparing several mainstream Istanbul options and wanting a clear, predictable, all-in-one process.One of the largest hair transplant providers in Turkey, serving a high volume of international patients each year.Large multidisciplinary medical team and dedicated international patient services.Elithair has built its reputation around accessibility, structured treatment pathways, and experience managing patients from around the world. The clinic offers FUE-based hair restoration procedures supported by multilingual coordinators, comprehensive treatment packages, and established pre- and post-operative processes. With consultation locations in several countries and a large operational infrastructure, it is often considered by patients looking for a streamlined and highly organised treatment experience.Streamlined, end-to-end care built around the international patient.A coordinated team specialising in travel-and-treatment packages for overseas patients.AHD Clinic delivers a smooth, fully managed journey from initial consultation through aftercare. Article Title: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella flips a Nobel economist's paradox on AI buyers; says: The seller learns more and more about you as you...
Article Text:
How Satya Nadella's Reverse Information Paradox flips Kenneth Arrow's economics

Why Satya Nadella wants AI distillation restrictions loosened for enterprises

Satya Nadella's five-point AI playbook: Control, Capability, Choice, Cost, Compound

Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella has a warning for every company racing to plug AI into its workflows: you might be handing over the very thing that makes you valuable. In an essay posted on X on Sunday, July 13, the Microsoft boss argued that businesses adopting AI tools are paying twice—once with money, and again with proprietary knowledge they reveal just to make those tools useful.He calls it the "Reverse Information Paradox," and it flips a decades-old idea from Nobel Prize-winning economist Kenneth Arrow. Arrow's original paradox described the seller's dilemma in the information market—you can't know what information is worth until you have it, at which point you've effectively got it for free. Nadella says AI reverses that risk entirely. Now it's the buyer who leaks value, correction by correction, prompt by prompt.Nadella's core point is that the more you want a model to perform, the more of your internal expertise you have to feed it. "You essentially pay for intelligence twice, once with money, and again with something even more valuable: the proprietary knowledge you must reveal to make that intelligence useful," he wrote.The asymmetry only widens with use. "The seller learns more and more about you as you use what you purchased, while you learn very little about what the seller is learning in return," Nadella said.Every prompt, correction, and evaluation gets distilled into institutional know-how—the kind a competitor could never buy, and the kind that leaks almost imperceptibly.The Microsoft chief also took aim at AI labs that restrict distillation, the training method used to build smaller, cheaper models. He finds it ironic that providers claim fair-use rights to train on public data, then impose tight terms on customers while reserving the right to learn from their usage. If learning only flows one way, he argued, value converges toward whoever owns the infrastructure rather than the people creating the knowledge. His remarks land months after Anthropic accused three Chinese firms—DeepSeek, Moonshot AI, and MiniMax—of running fake accounts to distill from its Claude model.Nadella wants an AI-era equivalent of patents—a way to use a model without surrendering ownership of what you learn through it. He lays out five moves: Control (own your evals, memory, and traces), Capability (build private training environments inside your own boundary), Choice (keep the orchestration layer independent of any single model), Cost (decouple to combine models efficiently), and Compound (bring it together into a continuous learning loop).The timing is pointed. AI bills are climbing as firms shift from flat subscriptions to usage-based pricing, and the "tokenmaxxing" era of treating heavy consumption as productivity is starting to bite.
Article Summary: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella calls it the "Reverse Information Paradox" In an essay posted on X on Sunday, July 13, he argued that businesses adopting AI are paying twice. The more you want a model to perform, the more of your internal expertise you have to feed it.

Article Title: Auto industry analysts are worried about Tesla and other car makers using the technology that Google, Apple, Samsung and other smartphone companies have been using since over ten years to update their
Article Text:
OTA updates raise cybersecurity concerns: Analyst



Norwegian bus test highlights potential risks



Auto makers are increasingly using over-the-air (OTA) software updates, a technology that smartphone companies like Google, Apple and Samsung have relied on for more than a decade to deliver new features and security fixes. While the technology has made updating vehicles faster and cheaper, cybersecurity experts are warning that it also creates new risks. According to a CNBC report, analysts say wireless updates could leave connected vehicles vulnerable to cyberattacks, data breaches and even attempts by foreign actors to interfere with vehicle systems.Over-the-air (OTA) technology allows manufacturers to wirelessly send software updates, bug fixes and new features to internet-connected devices without requiring a visit to a service center.Tesla began rolling out OTA updates for its Model S vehicles in 2012, helping popularize the technology across the automotive industry. Today, many car makers use OTA updates to improve vehicle software and fix issues remotely."The technology is increasingly welcomed as it is a quick and cost-effective way to manage systems on vehicles, over traditional methods which may have required a recall or update at routine maintenance," Siraj Ahmed Shaikh, professor in systems security at Swansea University in the UK, told CNBC.However, analysts say the growing use of OTA updates also introduces new cybersecurity risks.Its use represents "a unique national security concern," Gabriel Lim, senior analyst at the S. Rajaratnam School of International Studies in Singapore, told CNBC."Aside from data privacy concerns, the potential of a foreign actor sabotaging the controls of a moving vehicle is a possibility that countries like Norway, Denmark, and Britain have expressed concerns about," Lim added.The concerns gained attention after Norwegian public transport company Ruter tested two buses last year and found potential security risks linked to OTA technology."There is access to the control system for battery and power supply via mobile network through a Romanian SIM card. In theory, therefore, this bus can be stopped or rendered inoperable by the manufacturer," the company said.The findings prompted authorities in the UK and Denmark to launch their own investigations.
Article Summary: Auto makers are increasingly using over-the-air (OTA) software updates. The technology allows manufacturers to send software updates, bug fixes and new features to internet-connected devices without requiring a visit to a service center. But cybersecurity experts are warning that it also creates new risks.

Article Title: Engineers building Solar panels are using exact replicas of honeycombs made by Bees to ensure ...
Article Text:
How bee honeycombs could make solar panels more efficient



Tests show big gains in power outpuT

Bees may have helped solve one of solar energy's biggest problems. According to a report by Ecoportal, engineers have developed a new type of solar panel using the honeycomb pattern that bees have mastered for millions of years. The idea is simple. Traditional solar panels lose a lot of sunlight when the sun is not directly overhead because the light bounces off the surface. The new honeycomb-inspired design traps more of that light instead of reflecting it. Early tests suggest the approach could significantly improve power generation while making solar panels more useful on a wider range of surfaces.One of the biggest challenges with today's solar panels is that they work best only when sunlight falls directly on them. As the sun moves during the day, the light hits the panels at an angle. A large part of that light gets reflected instead of being turned into electricity.To fix this, engineers looked to nature for inspiration. They created a 3D concave solar panel based on the same hexagonal honeycomb structure built by bees. Instead of allowing sunlight to bounce away, the angled walls inside the structure make the light bounce from one surface to another.This gives the panel another chance to absorb the energy.According to the researchers quoted in the report, the new design increased maximum power output by 142.3% compared with a conventional flat solar panel under the same conditions. Even when sunlight hit the panel straight on, power output improved by 36.4%. At a 60-degree angle, where traditional panels usually struggle the most, performance increased by 61.8%.The team also used a flexible 3D polymer metamaterial to support the honeycomb structure. This means the design could eventually be used on curved buildings, electric vehicles and even aerospace equipment, instead of being limited to flat rooftops.
Article Summary: Engineers have developed a new type of solar panel using the honeycomb pattern that bees have mastered for millions of years. Traditional solar panels lose a lot of sunlight when the sun is not directly overhead because the light bounces off the surface. The new honeycomb-inspired design traps more of that light instead of reflecting it.

Article Title: Salesforce launches SMB growth kit for small businesses in India
Article Text: Salesforce has announced the SMB Growth Kit, a connected platform designed specifically for India’s small and medium businesses. The offering brings Agentforce Sales, Slack and Tableau together, helping SMBs replace disconnected tools with a unified system that improves visibility, accelerates collaboration and supports revenue growth. Salesforce says businesses can go live in four to six weeks.Agentforce Sales gives SMBs a real-time view of every lead, opportunity and pipeline, eliminating reliance on spreadsheets or manual updates. Automated lead assignment, workflow automation and mobile access help sales teams move faster. Built‑in AI capabilities — including opportunity scoring, forecasting and intelligent recommendations — allow teams to focus on high‑value opportunities without specialist expertise.Slack serves as the collaboration layer for SMBs adopting new technology. Already familiar to many growing businesses, Slack enables teams across sales, support, HR, IT and operations to work together in one place. For smaller teams where employees juggle multiple responsibilities, Slack helps save time and keep work moving efficiently.Tableau turns business data into actionable insights through live, mobile‑friendly dashboards.Founders and business leaders can monitor pipeline health, revenue trends and overall performance without waiting for static reports. Its drag‑and‑drop interface makes analytics accessible to all users, while native Salesforce connectivity provides real‑time visibility without complex integrations.Together, Agentforce Sales, Slack and Tableau form a single operating system that helps Indian SMBs sell smarter, collaborate faster and make confident decisions. The SMB Growth Kit includes structured onboarding, hands‑on setup and post‑go‑live support, enabling teams to adopt AI‑ready systems without operational burden. The offering will be delivered through select Salesforce reseller partners across India.Mankiran Chowhan, Managing Director, Salesforce India, said the challenge for SMBs is scattered customer data, conversations and insights. “SMB Growth Kit gives growing businesses one connected system where sales activity, team conversations and live insights work together — helping teams act with more confidence, fewer blind spots and less guesswork.”The SMB Growth Kit is available immediately for small and medium businesses in India through select Salesforce reseller partners. Salesforce teams will support customers with onboarding, setup and post‑go‑live assistance, with businesses expected to be live within four to six weeks.
Article Summary: Salesforce has announced the SMB Growth Kit, a connected platform designed specifically for India's small and medium businesses. The offering brings Agentforce Sales, Slack and Tableau together, helping SMBs replace disconnected tools with a unified system. Salesforce says businesses can go live in four to six weeks.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Nikola Tesla: 'If you want to find the secrets of the universe, think in terms of ...'
Article Text:
"If you want to find the

secrets of the universe

, think in terms of energy, frequency and vibration."

Quote of the day by Nikola Tesla

"If you want to find the secrets of the universe, think in terms of energy, frequency and vibration."

The meaning behind the quote

Why is the quote associated with Nikola Tesla

Why the quote continues to be shared

Nikola Tesla is often credited with one of the most widely shared quotes about understanding the universe through science:The statement continues to be referenced in discussions around physics, innovation and Tesla's approach to understanding the natural world.The quote reflects Tesla's belief that many natural phenomena can be understood by studying how energy behaves and how frequency and vibration influence physical systems. While the wording is frequently attributed to Tesla, it has been widely circulated over the years in books, speeches and online discussions.The quote suggests approaching the universe through measurable physical principles rather than appearances alone. In physics, energy is the ability to do work. Frequency is the number of times that something that repeats happens in a given amount of time. Vibration is an oscillating motion.Together, these concepts help explain phenomena ranging from sound and light to electricity and electromagnetic waves. The quote has therefore been interpreted as an encouragement to examine the fundamental forces that shape the physical world.Tesla was an inventor and electrical engineer whose work on alternating current (AC) power systems, electric motors, wireless transmission and high-frequency electrical experiments influenced the development of modern electrical engineering.Much of his research focused on electricity, magnetism and resonant systems, making the themes of energy, frequency and vibration closely associated with his scientific work.The statement is often quoted in discussions of science, engineering and innovation because it links complex physical ideas with a simple way of thinking about the natural world. Decades on, the quote is a staple of educational material, public talks and discussions of Tesla’s legacy, a testament to his enduring appeal as one of the great figures in electrical science.
Article Summary: "If you want to find the secrets of the universe, think in terms of energy, frequency and vibration" is one of the most widely shared quotes about understanding the universe through science. The statement is often quoted in discussions of science, engineering and innovation because it links complex physical ideas with a simple way of thinking about the natural world.

Article Title: After Moonshot’s Kimi K3, China’s Alibaba launches AI model ‘challenging’ Anthropic’s Fable 5
Article Text:
Alibaba

What is Alibaba's 2.4 trillion-parameter AI model



Win-win for Alibaba

China’s AI boom has just picked up speed as days after startup Moonshot AI rocked the tech world with its Kimi K3 system, e-commerce giant Alibaba launched a preview of its latest flagship model, Qwen3.8 Max. The tech giant positioned the powerful new model as directly comparable to top global frontier systems, describing its performance as second only to Anthropic’s industry-leading Fable 5. According to a report by Bloomberg, the back-to-back releases from Chinese companies signal that they are rapidly ‘erasing’ the capabilities gap with Silicon Valley.Alibaba rolled out preview access to Qwen3.8 Max, allowing software developers to immediately test the system through its coding platforms, including Qoder. While full technical specifications remain under wraps, Alibaba reportedly confirmed that Qwen3.8 Max boasts a massive 2.4 trillion parameters, which is the key metric measuring an AI’s internal scale and processing capability.The company also announced plans to release the model as an “open-weight” system soon, allowing outside developers to download and customise the code freely.The product rollout tops off a streak of high-profile wins for the e-commerce giant.Chinese regulators recently approved Apple Intelligence in the country, selecting Alibaba as Apple's primary technology partner to power local AI features for iPhone users across China. Alibaba’s release comes right on the heels of Moonshot AI’s announcement of Kimi K3, a massive system containing 2.8 trillion parameters.Backed by tech heavyweights Alibaba and Tencent, Moonshot hailed Kimi K3 as its "most capable flagship model to date." The startup confirmed that full access to the system, including its advanced coding, reasoning, and automated knowledge-work features, will officially open to the public on July 27.Upon its release, Kimi K3 will become the world's first open-source model in the 3-trillion-parameter class, allowing global researchers and businesses to run top-tier AI locally without relying on US cloud infrastructure.
Article Summary: Alibaba's Qwen3.8 Max boasts 2.4 trillion parameters, second only to Anthropic's Fable 5. Full access to the system, including its advanced coding, reasoning, and automated knowledge-work features, will officially open to the public on July 27.

Article Title: World's largest AI model repository Hugging Face says 'hacked' by AI Agents, says: Intrusion started where AI platforms are uniquely ...
Article Text:
Representative Image

Hugging Face explains how AI attack unfolded



‘ AI safety guardrails blocked defenders’



Hugging Face, the world’s largest open-source artificial intelligence (AI) repository, has revealed that it was hit by a sophisticated hacking attack driven entirely by an “AI agent”. Hugging Face has also disclosed that after the US-made models blocked requests to neutralise the attack due to guardrails, they used an open-weight AI model from China to mitigate the attack.According to Hugging Face, the breach began in a vulnerability specific to AI hosting infrastructure: the data-processing pipeline. A malicious dataset injected into the system exploited two separate code-execution flaws. Once inside, the autonomous AI agent escalated its access to gain control over internal server nodes, harvested security credentials and moved laterally across several internal server clusters over a weekend.Notably, it was the scale and speed of the hacking incident that caught the attention. According to Huggin Face, the hacker deployed an autonomous agent framework that executed thousands of coordinated actions across a fleet of temporary sandboxes.Secondly, the AI system dynamically shifted its command-and-control operations across public cloud services to evade traditional security filters. Hugging Face confirmed that while unauthorised access to a limited set of internal datasets and service credentials occurred, there is no evidence that public user-facing models, datasets or software packages were tampered with.“The intrusion started where AI platforms are uniquely exposed: the data-processing pipeline. A malicious dataset abused two code-execution paths in our dataset processing (a remote-code dataset loader and a template-injection in a dataset configuration) to run code on a processing worker,” the company said.When Hugging Face engineers rushed to analyse server logs, they hit a roadblock. The team initially tried using commercial, top-tier AI models via cloud APIs to analyse the malicious code. However, the safety guardrails built into these commercial AI services flagged the actual attack payloads as harmful content, instantly locking out the cybersecurity responders.To bypass the lockout, Hugging Face ran GLM 5.2 – an open-weight AI model by Chinese technology company Z.ai – locally on its own private infrastructure. This allowed defenders to analyse the malicious attack data freely while ensuring sensitive internal tokens never left their secure environment.“The attacker was bound by no usage policy, while our own forensic work was blocked by the guardrails of the hosted models we first tried,” Hugging Face noted, pointing out a critical gap in corporate cybersecurity.
Article Summary: Hugging Face, the world’s largest open-source artificial intelligence (AI) repository, has revealed that it was hit by a sophisticated hacking attack driven entirely by an “AI agent” Hugging Face has also disclosed that after the US-made models blocked requests to neutralise the attack due to guardrails, they used an open-weight AI model from China.
